# Tim Rumney appointed permanent chief executive of Best Western GB

Hotel 26 Nov 202111:35 by Katherine Price

bookmark_borderSave to Library

Hotelier Tim Rumney has been appointed full-time chief executive of BWH Hotel Group GB, incorporating Best Western Great Britain and WorldHotels Great Britain, having served as interim chief executive since August.

Rumney has been chairman of the BWH Hotel Group board of directors for four years and a director since 2009 and therefore “represented a natural and unanimous choice for the board” following the resignation of Rob Paterson earlier this year.

Rumney has operated three Best Western-branded hotels over more than 20 years including the Castle Green hotel in Kendal and the Best Western Plus Pinewood hotel in Wilmslow. He also previously held senior positions with Forte Hotels.

He said: “I am thrilled to take on the position of CEO full time at BWH Hotel Group GB, in a time of great challenge and great opportunity for the hospitality industry.

“Working in hospitality and running a hospitality business is a difficult and challenging, but supremely rewarding career choice. As CEO I will take all the lessons I have learned in my career forward to fight our corner, and ensure our collective voice is heard.”

“We have a fantastic team in BWH Hotel Group GB and our purchasing organisation Beacon and I look forward to working with our hotel members and other stakeholders in driving and developing the business as we recover from the impact of the pandemic.”

BWH Group GB is retained-profit membership organisation of more than 290 independent hotels, with 18 hotel brands.

Best Western Hotel Hotels

Tim Rumney

“There is only one boss. The Guest. And he can fire everybody in the company from the chairman on down, simply by spending his money somewhere else”.

Tim Rumney

# Welcome

Like many people I fell into the hotel industry by accident after taking a holiday job. Following a summer spent working and playing hard, I had a decision to make about whether this was going to be my career or if I was going to follow my father into journalism. The hotel won and I spent the next two years as a trainee manager working in all departments of the hotel. After attending university and blagging a placement at the Capital Hotel in Knightsbridge, I moved on to the Forte Hotel Group. The rest as they say, is history.

---

I loved running my consultancy and would like to thank all the clients and teams that I worked with during that time. I have now set off on a new challenge, as CEO of BWH Hotel Group GB. I believe that this is the best home for independent hotel owners and operators in the country and we are well into our strategy to support our existing hotels and grow the brand. Come and talk to me if you want to part of this exciting adventure and for all my previous clients, good luck and goodbye for now…

# BWH Hotels GB CEO Tim Rumney speaks on plight of hospitality

By Darren Greenwood Business Editor

Share

0 Comments

Tim Rumney (Image: Pic supplied)

This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

The boss of a York-based hotel franchise has warned many hotels are stopping recruitment, investments and will hike prices.

Tim Rumney, CEO of BWH Hotels GB, says more than four-fifths of the 230 hotels in the chain are already cutting staff hours to cope with rising costs.

Mr Rumney called it encouraging inflation had dropped to 2.8 per cent in February but said the Chancellor’s Spring Statement “does little to ease the pressures on independent hoteliers.”

He said: “The reality is, with the UK economy shrinking by 0.1 per cent in January and interest rates stuck at 4.5 per cent, many hotels will have no choice but to stop recruiting, cancel investments and hike prices.

RECOMMENDED READING:

- York Central MP Rachael Maskell responds to Spring Statement
- West & North Yorkshire Chamber responds to Reeves statement
- Kevin Hollinrake responds to Chancellor's Spring Statement
- Luke Charters speaks on Spring Statement from Rachel Reeves

“Not an obvious formula to reach the Chancellors reduced growth targets - and the last thing hoteliers want to do.

Mr Rumney explained: “While the proposed increase in household disposable income may offer some hope, the ongoing freeze on income tax thresholds negates this.

“As wage increases push more into higher tax brackets without a real boost in spending power, limiting disposable income for travel.

“This results in guests booking last minute, which is making forecasting harder than ever.”

Within his partner hotels, the percentage of bookers reserving within a day of arrival had jumped ten per cent, and by six per cent for those in under seven days, giving hoteliers less confidence in forecasts and being unable to plan.

Mr Rumney continued: “While no immediate tax rises may provide short-term stability, spending cuts could further hit consumer confidence and spending.

“April 1st will also bring higher employer costs, with National Insurance hikes and wage increases further squeezing hotel payrolls. The new Employment Rights Bill also brings the prospect of higher costs and more red-tape, making job creation less attractive.

“A recent survey of more than 230 of our BWH hoteliers revealed 83 per cent are already cutting staff hours to cope with rising costs.

“Despite calls for VAT reductions, business reform and NICs exemptions for lower-band taxpayers from more than 70 per cent of our hoteliers, the pressure continues to mount. Without real support, our industry is left to face these challenges by ourselves.”

Mr Rumney called on hoteliers to partner with BWH to maintain their independence, whilst still receiving support.

He added: “With the government tightening its grip on spending, hotels must adapt fast. While the Spring Statement won’t provide the help many hoped for, with the right support, independent hoteliers can still find ways to succeed.”

# BWH Hotels GB reports seven per cent revenue increase H1 2025

- August 20, 2025
- Eloise Hanson

Facebook

Twitter

LinkedIn

Email

Save for later

Go to your saved post page

[Credit: The Clarence Gardens Hotel, Best Western Signature Collection]

- Tim Rumney, BWH Hotels GB, Jim Muir

Register Here

Reading Time: 2 minutes

UK: Independent collection BWH Hotels GB has reported a seven per cent revenue increase in the first half of the year, between January and June 2025.

The group delivered £73.3 million in total bedroom revenue, representing a seven per cent increase on 2024.

Meetings, events and group bookings are up 19 per cent year-on-year. This is credited to the group’s dedicated venues team and launch of Planners Advantage, an extension of BWH’s global BW Rewards programme to M&E organisers.

Its rewards programme also continues to grow, with members spending £30.6 million in BWH hotels in the first half of 2025. This is a 17 per cent increase over 2024 and 11 per cent ahead of budget.

Jim Muir, BWH Hotels commercial director, said: “At the heart of everything we do is a simple principle: helping our hotels thrive. That’s why we invest in a dedicated sales team, digital marketing and high-impact campaigns like the Year of the Free Breakfast. When our hotels succeed, we all succeed.”

During the same period, BWH GB welcomed nine hotels to the collection including The Clarence Gardens Hotel in Scarborough, Velvet Hotel in Manchester, and The Crooklands in Kendal, with more additions to the BW Signature Collection anticipated soon.

Tim Rumney, CEO of BWH Hotels GB said: “We’ve made a strong start to 2025 and the second half of the year brings fresh challenges and opportunities. Hotels are still facing real pressure from cautious corporate demand to rising costs and continued economic uncertainty.

“At BWH, we’re not just responding to those challenges, we’re helping our hotels get ahead of them. From optimising digital performance to driving direct bookings and boosting loyalty, everything we do is designed to deliver real returns where it counts.

“Moving forward, we are continuing our focus on stimulating corporate demand, optimising the performance of our new website, investing in digital channels to better drive conversions on behalf of our hotels and delivering continued value through our sales, marketing and loyalty programmes. Thanks to our incredible hotel partners and hardworking teams, we’re confident about what’s to come,” he added.

##### Highlights:

• BWH Hotels GB reported a seven per cent revenue increase in H1 2025, totalling £73.3 million.

• Meetings and events bookings grew 19 per cent, boosted by the Planners Advantage programme.

• The BW Rewards programme rose 17 per cent, generating £30.6 million in member spend.

• Nine new hotels joined the BWH GB collection, expanding its UK footprint.

• Strategic focus remains on digital optimisation, direct bookings, loyalty, and corporate demand growth.

evidence.title: “We are focusing on driving more revenue and more brand contribution to our members” BWH Hotels GB details five-year strategy
evidence.fetched_text:
“We are focusing on driving more revenue and more brand contribution to our members” BWH Hotels GB details five-year strategy

BWH Hotels GB has been a staple of British hospitality for more than 70 years.

As the largest collection of independent hotels in Great Britain, the portfolio comprises more than 230 properties from historic castles to coaching inns and modern city hotels.

Each property is individually owned, and is actively encouraged to retain this fierce sense of independence.

BWH Hotels passionately champions ‘The Power of Two’, bringing support to its members across revenue, sales and marketing segments, as well as strength and solidarity of the brand.

This year, it laid out bold ambitions to add 100 hotels to its GB portfolio as part of a five-year strategy.

The York-based British arm of the business, which encompasses Best Western and the luxury brand WorldHotels, unveiled plans to add 8,000 rooms over the next five years, building on the momentum after an ‘exceptionally strong’ 2024.

For the first time, at the start of April, BWH Hotels came together for its debut Pan European Conference, hosting over 1,200 member hoteliers from every corner of the globe in Palma across an inspiring three-day event.

Each country held its own programme of events, before coming together on the final two days to share insights from across EMEA and learn how BWH Hotels is working to bring more business to its members.

On the line-up for BWH Hotels GB was commercial director Jim Muir, who shared with members a frank and honest account of the company’s performance over the past 12 months.

Despite the flat start to the year, as experienced by the majority of the hospitality industry, Muir is confident that the brand’s ability to drive more revenue into its member hotels is strengthening.

“We feel that we’re doing the right things, we’re on the right track and that we are going to deliver a good year,” he said. “Despite the fact that there’s a lot of pessimism around the industry and the mood of the country, we feel really positive about the changes we are implementing in the business.”

The changes he references are part of the brand’s five-year plan and the roll out of two major improvements that have already been implemented.

The first was increasing the commission paid to corporate travel agents, which Muir explained, has “strengthened our position and ensured we remain competitive. Driving more revenue through corporate travel agents is a key priority.”

Looking ahead to the end of the year, business for member hotels looks strong across corporate and leisure segments. The level of business on the books for M&E from April through to December is up 72% YOY. It’s a similarly strong story for leisure group bookings, with 10% more confirmed revenue for the rest of the year already. “I can speak with absolute confidence that it’s going to be a really strong year for M&E,” Muir said, “and we are continuing to build on what was a really strong 2024 for leisure groups.”

The big breakfast

A second major change was introducing the Year of the Free Breakfast campaign. The incentive offered free breakfast to all Rewards customers who booked the ‘Free Breakfast’ rate directly through the Best Western website. The model worked by removing the cash discount on prepaid rates and instead offering consumers something that has a much higher perceived value.

The campaign was brought to life through a number of channels, with more investment into marketing than ever before. Promotions ran across commercial radio and digital channels, as well as adverts on billboards, backed by a big nationwide PR push.

The aim was to be more visible, capture more business and drive more direct bookings to Best Western hotels.

“We wanted to give people a really good reason to choose a Best Western hotel over any other brands,” said Muir. “And of course, we also wanted to drive channel shift to our direct channels too.”

“We’ve been more visible to consumers and to potential consumers than we’ve ever been before and we have invested more money in marketing than we have ever done before.”

A key objective was to try and attract a new audience to the hotels. Investment is consistently ploughed into fine tuning the way the brand secures bookings from new guests and the Free Breakfast campaign was a core driver of this.

Muir said: “The percentage of revenue which is delivered from new users is increasing by 50% year on year. The website conversion rate, so the percentage of website visitors who have gone to book, is up 13%, so we aren’t just driving more users to the website, but to actually book as well.

“We’ve seen booked revenues through Best Western upper UK segment increase by 9% year on year since the start of February as well, which has been really encouraging to see.” In another major development, the brand is working hard on a new website, which is due to go live later this year. As a result, the Best Western GB loyalty program will be relaunched and will make a ‘huge difference’.

“Last year, the loyalty programme brought in £60m of revenue, which was up 9% year on year,” Muir explained.

“Having a better-performing and more modern loyalty programme will really help drive repeat bookings, encouraging customers to prioritise staying again with one of our properties.”

Growth plans

BWH Hotels has ambitious growth plans and is actively looking for quality hotels to onboard in key UK locations. After a strong 2024, with successes including adding its first long-stay property, the Liverpool Dockside Aparthotel, the brand is keen to secure more partnerships with hotel owners looking to turbocharge their growth whilst keeping their character intact.

BWH Hotels GB has a total of 16 brands across three main pillars, including WorldHotels and Sure Stay Hotels, with footholds in every segment from economy to luxury to boutique and extended stay. The goal now is to try to re-establish the wider Best Western brand as a more luxury offering and communicate this clearly to guests.

“Our brand has stretched very significantly in recent years. But our brand is best known as Best Western, and Best Western is generally perceived to be mid-market,” explained Muir.

“The way that we communicate about our brand hasn’t really evolved in line with the growth of the individual sub brands, and this is a real challenge for us.

“Our global research has shown if people understand the brand more, if they understand all of the sub brands within it, they are much more likely to consider booking and using that brand. It is a challenge but it has real potential to enable us to drive much more revenue into our hotels.”

To find out more about the advantages of joining forces with BWH Hotels, visit www.joinbwhhotels.co.uk

This story was part of a paid partnership with BWH Hotels.

# “Some PMS providers make you feel like a number” Best Western GB, CEO on choosing the right tech
by[Zoe Monk](https://www.boutiquehotelier.com/author/zoemonk/)September 17, 2020September 17, 2020
[**TW]()[**FB](http://www.facebook.com/share.php?u=https://www.boutiquehotelier.com/some-pms-providers-make-you-feel-like-a-number-best-western-gb-ceo-on-choosing-the-right-tech/)[**LN](http://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?url=https://www.boutiquehotelier.com/some-pms-providers-make-you-feel-like-a-number-best-western-gb-ceo-on-choosing-the-right-tech/)[**WHATSAPP](https://api.whatsapp.com/send?text=https://www.boutiquehotelier.com/some-pms-providers-make-you-feel-like-a-number-best-western-gb-ceo-on-choosing-the-right-tech/)[**MAIL]()
![Best Western Rob Paterson](https://www.boutiquehotelier.com/2019/12/Rob-Paterson-x.jpg "“Some PMS providers make you feel like a number” Best Western GB, CEO on choosing the right tech 1")
**Rob Paterson, CEO at Best Western GB is confident that his choice of PMS system will support the brand’s growth and improve the customer experience across all hotels in the portfolio.**
Guestline serves over 80% of its UK properties and is proving to be a reliable property management system as Best Western GB steps up its footprint and builds its presence across its 17 brands.
Rob Paterson, chief executive officer at Best Western GB, cites the ongoing, knowledgeable support as one of the reasons the brand chooses Guestline. Paterson says: “Our hoteliers are passionate independent hoteliers, and what they really value is the personal experience, the responsiveness.
“I think with some PMS vendors you can feel like a number. You can go in a queue and you might not hear a response for three weeks, three months. I think that’s why they select a product like Guestline. It’s very much a product where, if they pick up the phone, they know that they’re going to get hold of somebody and they know the product is reliable.”
**Seamless integration**
As well as these benefits, integration is seamless, with Guestline’s software integrating with Best Western’s central reservation system to create an automated, two-way interface which saves hoteliers lots of time and admin.
Paterson added: “I have worked with a number of PMSs throughout my career and when you start to stack them up alongside each other and do a simple, objective ‘this is what I’m looking for’, Guestline ticks so many boxes. It’s a reliable, good value PMS, and that responsiveness, integrated side of things is really, really important.”
**Time saving technology**
Ensuring the technology supplier fits the hotel’s needs is vital to keep a hotel running smoothly. Consistency of tech and platforms is vital, too.
“We’d be kidding ourselves if we didn’t think technology was vital in today’s marketplace,” says Jim Muir, head of marketing at Best Western. “From as simple as optimising the website, making sure we drive as many visitors in to book us through our website, to looking at more niche bits of technology such as Upsell Guru, which has an integration with Guestline, through to the ramp platform that we’re developing for our members.
“Anything that can give [hoteliers] more time to spend dealing with the guests and giving them a brilliant experience has got to be a benefit for the individual hotel and the brand as a whole.”
To watch the full video interview with Rob Paterson, please click[here](https://vimeo.com/426914245/31cf7b7b8e).

5 Minutes With… Nicola Wood, Head of Sales at BWH Hotels - Event Organisers Summit

Thyme

Branchout

# 5 Minutes With… Nicola Wood, Head of Sales at BWH Hotels

- November 25, 2024
- Contact

In the latest instalment of our events industry executive interview series we spoke to Nicola Wood (pictured), Head of Sales at BWH Hotels, about the need for flexibility and personalisation when it comes to meeting rooms, the growing move towards sustainable solutions, the importance of instant online booking systems and more…

Tell us about your company, products and services.

BWH Hotels may seem a new name to many of you, but we have actually been in the UK for 50 plus years. If I was to say Best Western you would all know who we were. The name BWH Hotels was formed after the acquisition of World Hotels. BWH Hotels now have a wide portfolio of global brands and all our properties are independently owned and operated, meaning clients get much more opportunity to personalise their experiences.

We’re always looking for new ways to improve and expand on our offering, for example we recently launched Instant Book – a platform that allows you to book simple meetings (of 30 or less delegates) online, in an instant! But we’re not just about making meetings easy – we offer something out of the ordinary. With a mix of amazing locations and inspiring spaces, our carefully curated collection of hotels really do pack a punch.

What have been the biggest challenges the Events industry has faced over the past 12 months?

I’m sure everyone has heard it all before, but for us the lack of staffing across the venues is still causing problems – gone are the days where a GM was office based, now they may be cleaning a bedroom or turning meeting rooms around.

And what have been the biggest opportunities?

Clients want flexibility and opportunity to personalise events and meetings more – face to face time with clients/teams have become invaluable over the last few years and personalisation has become huge.

What is the biggest priority for the Events industry this year?

Sustainability and the ever-growing trend to becoming more sustainable. I just came back from the States, we were at a conference for 2500 people and everything was single use plastics. I’m pleased that the UK seem to be ahead of the curb with this, but there is so much more we can do.

What technology is going to have the biggest impact on the market this coming year?

Instant Booking for small meetings. Time is off the essence for PAs/EAs and even agents, being able to book simple meetings online is definitely the way we all need to move forward. Fortunately for us, we already have over 100 venues in the UK offering this facility.

You go to the bar at the Event Organisers Summit – what’s your tipple of choice?

Very boring, but for me you can’t beat a classic G&T or a glass of cold Sancerre.

What’s the most exciting thing about your job?

Every day is different, you never know what challenge or joy is going to come in

And what’s the most challenging?

Probably the same thing, like in any role you could have something scheduled, and something comes in which just needs your attention, and things get put on the back burner

What’s the best piece of advice you’ve ever been given? Do not to take things personally

Succession or Stranger Things? Neither I’m a rom-com kind of girl, so Emily in Paris for sure.

BOOK YOUR PLACE AT THE eCommerce FORUM

-- ABOUT | Nicola Wood

top of page

# ABOUT

Nicola is a creative force of nature. Her positivity and down to earth no-nonsense approach delivers world class creativity. She's all about insightful and disruptive ideas and finding the best way for them to land in culture - in whatever media is best.

She is a multi award-winning writer, CD and ECD - among the highest ranked in the world. In March 2024 she ranked in both the WARC and DRUM worldwide rankings and helped to rank Ogilvy UK as the top 5 creative agency in the world. She left Ogilvy in January with a 100% pitch record. Among her many recent wins, is the coveted D&AD White Pencil and Cannes Glass Lion awards for the culturally impactful "Have a word" integrated campaign for Mayor of London. The cheeky "Hornicultural Society" campaign for charity Relate was one of the world most awarded campaigns and featured in the 2024 WARC Creative 100 at no.38. Nicola has featured in the worldwide rankings for the last four consecutive years. In 2024 she ranked in the D&AD top 50 ECDs, in 2023 she was D&AD top 5 Copywriters worldwide and the in the top 20 Creative Director Worldwide. Nicola is in the 2024 Creativepool Creative Leader 100, was a 2022 Female Frontier Honouree shortlisted for the Conquering Creativity Award and was also awarded Creative Equals Future Leaders Award 2021. ​​ She has a track record for cracking tough briefs and winning pitches for both local and global clients, and has worked on some of Britain's best-loved brands including M&S, BBC and British Airways. She recently worked globally across Jameson Whiskey, and locally across Molson Coors brands, St James Place and the Mayor of London. She has a reputation for her love of craft and human insight. Bringing fun and truth to the creative department, as well as every brief, client relationship and team she works with. Nicola is focused on the people and the work because nurturing both talent and ideas makes her heart sing. She has always been hugely passionate about helping emerging talent come into the industry. Nicola's favourite human is her son Ollie and she loves being a Mum (even in the current teenage years).

# BWH Hotels Appoints Jade Humphrey as New Hotel Development Sales Director to Drive Strategic Growth in the UK

Published on October 31, 2025

Image generated with Ai

BWH Hotels GB has announced the appointment of Jade Humphrey as the new hotel development sales director. This pivotal role will see Humphrey steering the growth and development of BWH Hotels GB’s impressive range of hotel brands, which include Best Western, WorldHotels and SureStay. His expertise will be integral in supporting hotel owners, operators, investors and management companies as they align with the company’s global network and resources.

### Experienced Leadership for Strategic Expansion

With nearly three decades of experience in hospitality, sales transformation, project management and change delivery, Jade Humphrey brings a wealth of knowledge to his new role. His career spans several industries, including hospitality, aviation, healthcare, sport and film. Humphrey has previously held senior management and consultant positions, where he demonstrated a proven ability to drive growth, revenue and organizational change.

Prior to joining BWH Hotels GB, Humphrey worked with both independent hotels and global hotel brands, providing strategic insight and delivering transformation across multiple sectors. His experience in these diverse industries is set to bring valuable perspectives to the hospitality sector, as BWH Hotels continues to strengthen its market position.

### A Focus on Brand Flexibility and Partner Support

As the hotel development sales director, Humphrey will be responsible for ensuring that BWH Hotels GB’s extensive brand portfolio offers flexibility and value to hotel owners and operators. The company’s brands are designed to provide partners with the opportunity to maintain the unique character of their hotels while benefiting from the strength of a globally recognized brand.

Advertisement

Advertisement

The strategic direction under Humphrey’s leadership will focus on building strong partnerships with hotel owners, operators and management companies. His role will be key in facilitating the continued expansion of BWH Hotels across the UK, as well as strengthening relationships with its existing members.

Humphrey expressed his excitement about joining the team at a crucial time in the company’s growth. According to his statement, BWH Hotels provides an exceptional range of soft and hard brands that offer true flexibility for hotel partners. The brands’ flexibility allows operators to retain the individuality of their properties while leveraging the strength and recognition of BWH Hotels’ global network.

Advertisement

Advertisement

He is eager to support hotel owners and operators in aligning with the BWH Hotels network, enabling them to enhance their business and offer an exceptional guest experience under the umbrella of a proven and trusted brand. His emphasis will be on fostering long-term relationships that deliver measurable success for both partners and the company.

### A Vision for Transformational Growth

Robert Alley, the brand development director at BWH Hotels GB, also commented on the appointment, emphasizing the significant impact Jade Humphrey’s leadership will have on the company’s future growth. Alley noted that Humphrey’s extensive background in hospitality, combined with his ability to drive transformation, would play a vital role in supporting the expansion of BWH Hotels in the region.

Advertisement

Advertisement

Humphrey’s leadership is expected to help BWH Hotels GB solidify its presence in the UK market, driving business growth through new partnerships and strengthening the company’s existing relationships with hotel operators. His commitment to delivering growth and transformation will be a key driver in BWH Hotels’ ongoing success and its mission to provide exceptional value to its global network of hotel members.

### BWH Hotels GB’s Growing Influence

BWH Hotels GB, part of BWH Hotel Group, represents a portfolio of globally recognized hotel brands, including Best Western, WorldHotels and SureStay. The company’s portfolio continues to expand, offering hotel owners a broad spectrum of brand options, each designed to meet the diverse needs of today’s hospitality landscape.

Through its global network, BWH Hotels provides partners with access to extensive resources, including marketing support, reservation systems and operational expertise. This allows hotel owners to focus on delivering exceptional guest experiences while benefiting from the strength of a globally recognized brand.

With Humphrey’s appointment, BWH Hotels GB aims to build on its already strong foundation in the UK market, offering hotel owners new opportunities for growth and partnership. Humphrey’s strategic vision and experience will play a central role in driving the company’s continued success as it navigates the evolving landscape of the hospitality industry.

### Conclusion

The appointment of Jade Humphrey as hotel development sales director represents a significant step forward for BWH Hotels GB as it continues to expand and strengthen its position in the UK hospitality market. Humphrey’s extensive experience, coupled with his commitment to driving growth and transformation, will ensure that BWH Hotels remains a trusted partner for hotel owners and operators across the region.

With a focus on flexibility, value and strategic partnership, Humphrey is poised to lead BWH Hotels GB into a new phase of growth, helping to shape the future of the company’s hotel network and offering continued success for its partners.

- November 25, 2024
- Contact

In the latest instalment of our events industry executive interview series we spoke to Nicola Wood (pictured), Head of Sales at BWH Hotels, about the need for flexibility and personalisation when it comes to meeting rooms, the growing move towards sustainable solutions, the importance of instant online booking systems and more…

Tell us about your company, products and services.

BWH Hotels may seem a new name to many of you, but we have actually been in the UK for 50 plus years. If I was to say Best Western you would all know who we were. The name BWH Hotels was formed after the acquisition of World Hotels.
